back_translation: After this, Jesus revealed Himself to the disciples again at the Sea of Tiberias. He revealed Himself in this way.
Simon Peter, Thomas (called Didymus), Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, the sons of Zebedee, and two other of His disciples were together.
Simon Peter said to them, “I am going fishing.” They answered, “We will go with you too.” So they went out and got into the boat, and that night they caught nothing.
When the day broke, Jesus came and stood on the shore, but the disciples did not know it was Him.
So Jesus said to them, “Children, do you have any fish?” They answered, “No.”
He said to them, “Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and you will get some.” So they cast the net, and could not pull it up because the fish were so many.

Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, “That is the Lord!” When Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he grabbed his outer garment and tied it around his waist, for he was naked, and jumped into the sea.
The other disciples came in the boat and dragged the net full of fish, for they were not far from the shore, only about 90 meters away.
When they got ashore, they saw a charcoal fire there, with fish lying on it, and bread.
Jesus said to them, “Bring some of the fish you have just caught.”
So Simon Peter went up onto the boat and dragged the net to shore. The net was full of large fish, 153 of them. And although there were so many, the net was not torn.
Jesus said to them, “Come, let us eat breakfast.” None of the disciples dared to ask Him, “Who are You?” because they knew it was the Lord.
Jesus came and took the bread and gave it to them, and likewise the fish.
This was now the third time Jesus revealed Himself to the disciples after He had risen from the dead.

When they had finished eating, Jesus said to Simon Peter, “Simon son of John, do you love Me more than these?” Peter said to Him, “Yes, Lord, You know that I love You.” Jesus said to him, “Feed My lambs.”
He said to him a second time, “Simon son of John, do you love Me?” Peter said to Him, “Yes, Lord, You know that I love You.” Jesus said to him, “Tend My sheep.”

He said to him a third time, “Simon son of John, do you love Me?” Peter was grieved that He asked him a third time, “Do you love Me?” He said to Him, “Lord, You know all things, You know that I love You.” Jesus said to him, “Feed My sheep.

Truly, truly, I say to you, when you were young, you tied your own waist and walked where you wished. But when you grow old, you will stretch out your hands, and another will tie your waist for you and lead you to where you do not want to go.”
He said this to indicate the kind of death by which Peter would glorify God. After saying this, He said to him, “Follow Me.”
Peter turned and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved following them. This was the one who had leaned against Him at the meal and asked, “Lord, who is the one who will betray You?”
When Peter saw him, he asked Jesus, “Lord, what about this one?”
Jesus said to him, “If I want him to remain until I come back, what is that to you? You — follow Me.”

So a rumor spread among the brothers that this disciple would not die. But Jesus had not said to Peter that he would not die; He had only said, “If I want him to remain until I come back, what is that to you?”
This is the disciple who is a witness to these events and who has recorded them, and we know that his testimony is true.
There are still many other things that Jesus did. If they were all written down one by one, I suppose the whole world could not contain the books that would be written.
